mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating consumer spending power, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances or trigger frequency drops below 2 events per year.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital investment demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases or interaction term loses statistical significance in OLS regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of low inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization without pricing pressure, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL exceeds 0.3% monthly growth, or 20-day returns are negative in >55% of regime-matched observations.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll employment gains above 150,000 confirm economic momentum and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of months with PAYEMS gains exceeding 150,000 or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level below 0.5% combined with persistent deflationary pressure forces the central bank to maintain ultra-loose policy, sustaining equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.5% and CPIAUCSL declines for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: 60-day returns turn negative in >55% of triggered periods or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 100 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling retail sales indicates weak consumer transmission of macro growth, suppressing corporate earnings expectations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in >60% of divergence episodes or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production contraction during high unemployment regimes signals severe demand destruction and factory idle capacity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >50% of triggered observations or the mechanism fails to generate a negative Rank IC greater than -0.04 over any 2-year window.
